content.thinking:
Good. I can see:
- 3-member MongoDB replica set (mongodb-replica-0/1/2) running in `mongodb` namespace
- MongoDB service `mongodb-replica-svc` is headless (ClusterIP: None) on port 27017
- Prometheus is running in `monitoring` namespace with `--web.enable-lifecycle` flag (so I can do a hot reload via `/-/reload`)
- Prometheus config only scrapes itself

The plan:
1. Deploy a mongodb-exporter standalone deployment in the `mongodb` namespace (or monitoring namespace)
2. Expose it via a Service
3. Update the Prometheus config to scrape it
4. Reload Prometheus

I'll use the Percona MongoDB exporter since it's well-established. For the MongoDB URI, I can connect to the replica set through the headless service or directly to individual pods. First, I need to check whether MongoDB has authentication enabled.

I got `switched to db admin;` but no actual count output. This might be a replica set issue - localhost exception may not work on a secondary or in a replica set configuration.

Actually wait - `usersInfo` returned an authentication error, which means there ARE users (otherwise localhost exception would work). But logs show UserNotFound for the users we tried... Unless the localhost exception isn't active because there are already users in the admin database, but the ones we're trying don't exist.

Let me check the earlier logs to see if any users were actually created.

The admin username is `admin-user` (not `admin` or `root`). Let me try authenticating with `admin-user` and the `admin123password` password.

content.text: The admin username is `admin-user`. Let me verify and then create a monitoring user.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh admin --host 127.0.0.1 --username admin-user --password admin123password --eval "printjson(db.runCommand({usersInfo:1}))" 2>&1 | head -30
input.description: Authenticate as admin-user

content.text: Now I'll create a monitoring user and deploy the mongodb-exporter.

input.description: Create mongodb-exporter monitoring user

content.text: Good. Now I'll create the mongodb-exporter Secret, Deployment, and Service in one go, then update Prometheus config.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter-creds
  namespace: mongodb
type: Opaque
stringData:
  mongodb-uri: "mongodb://mongodb-exporter:exporterpassword@mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017/admin?replicaSet=mongodb-replica&authSource=admin"
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
  labels:
    app: mongodb-exporter
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-exporter
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mongodb-exporter
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: mongodb-exporter
        image: percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40
        args:
          - "--mongodb.uri=$(MONGODB_URI)"
          - "--collect-all"
          - "--compatible-mode"
        env:
        - name: MONGODB_URI
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: mongodb-exporter-creds
              key: mongodb-uri
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9216
          name: metrics
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 50m
            memory: 64Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 128Mi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
  labels:
    app: mongodb-exporter
spec:
  selector:
    app: mongodb-exporter
  ports:
  - name: metrics
    port: 9216
    targetPort: 9216
EOF
input.description: Deploy mongodb-exporter with Secret, Deployment, and Service

content.content:
secret/mongodb-exporter-creds created
deployment.apps/mongodb-exporter created
service/mongodb-exporter created

tool_use_result.stdout:
secret/mongodb-exporter-creds created
deployment.apps/mongodb-exporter created
service/mongodb-exporter created

content.text: Now update the Prometheus config to scrape the mongodb-exporter:

input.command:
kubectl patch configmap prometheus-config -n monitoring --type merge -p '
{
  "data": {
    "prometheus.yml": "global:
  scrape_interval: 30s
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "mongodb"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["mongodb-exporter.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:9216"]
"
  }
}'
input.description: Update Prometheus config to add MongoDB scrape target

The percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40 has a DSN parsing issue. The URI format might be different. Let me check what format this version expects.

Actually, it says "scheme must be 'mongodb' or 'mongodb+srv'", which might mean the URI is somehow malformed. Let me check if the `--collect-all` and `--compatible-mode` flags are causing issues with this version.

For percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40, the URI should be standard MongoDB connection string. Let me try a simpler URI first - just connecting to the primary pod.
Maybe the replica set connection string is causing the parsing error, so I should test with a basic connection string instead.

content.text: The percona exporter has URI parsing issues with the replica set syntax. Let me fix it with a simpler primary-only URI:

input.command: kubectl patch secret mongodb-exporter-creds -n mongodb --type='json' -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/data/mongodb-uri","value":"'$(echo -n "mongodb://mongodb-exporter:exporterpassword@mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017/admin?authSource=admin" | base64 -w0)'"}]'

if [ -z "$PROM_POD" ]; then
  PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pods -n monitoring | grep '^prometheus-[a-f0-9]' | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
fi
echo "Prometheus pod: $PROM_POD"
kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- --post-data='' http://localhost:9090/-/reload && echo "Reload OK"

content.thinking:
Good. The Prometheus deployment mounts `prometheus-config` ConfigMap as a volume at `/etc/prometheus/`. The reload API should trigger a re-read of the config file from disk, but Kubernetes volumes from ConfigMaps don't update instantly - there's a sync delay of up to 2 minutes.

Wait, actually when you do `/-/reload`, Prometheus re-reads the config file from disk. The ConfigMap volume should have been updated by now (we did the patch a few minutes ago). Let me check what file is actually on disk in the Prometheus pod.

input.command:
PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pods -n monitoring | grep '^prometheus-[a-f0-9]' | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- cat /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml 2>&1
input.description: Check what prometheus.yml is currently on disk in the pod

PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pods -n monitoring | grep '^prometheus-[a-f0-9]' | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
until kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets" 2>&1 | grep -q "mongodb"; do

kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets" 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | grep -E "scrapeUrl|health|lastError" | head -20

content.text: MongoDB target appeared with `health: unknown` (first scrape hasn't completed yet). Let me wait for the first scrape to complete:

input.command:
PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pods -n monitoring | grep '^prometheus-[a-f0-9]' | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
until kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets" 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | grep -A2 "mongodb-exporter" | grep -q '"health": "up"'; do
  sleep 5
done
echo "MongoDB target is UP"
kubectl exec -n monitoring "$PROM_POD" -- wget -qO- "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=mongodb_up" 2>&1
input.description: Wait for mongodb target to become healthy and query mongodb_up
